content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 21, 1992

Ron Bearce
P.O. Box 4134
San Luis Obispo, CA  93403

Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-92-298

Dear Mr. Bearce:

     This is in response to your letter requesting general
assistance with respect to campaign provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹  You asked for general assistance
concerning filing requirements of the Act.²

     Please note that, as we have stated in past advice letters,
the Act requires that the Commission provide written advice to
persons whose duties under the Act are in question.  The
Commission will not provide advice to third parties concerning
another person's duties under the Act.  (Section 83114(b);
Regulation 18329(c).)  In addition, nothing in this letter
should be construed to evaluate any conduct which may have already
taken place.

     Moreover, since Commission advice is the application of the
law to a particular set of facts, you should be aware that the
same conclusion may not necessarily result where the facts differ
(such as where the source of the mailer or the content of the
mailer is different than the mailer you have submitted.)  For
example, a significant line of cases have distinguished materials
discussing measures issued by public agencies which are primarily

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800  ●  P.O. Box 807  ●  Sacramento, CA  95812  ●  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-92-298
Page 2


informational, rather than political. (See e.g., Penal Code
Section 424; Stanson v. Mott (1976) 17 Cal.3d 206; People v. Sperl
(1976) 54 Cal.App.3d 640; and People v. Battin (1978) 77
Cal.App.3d 635.) These other provisions of law, however, are
outside the Commission's jurisdiction and are not pertinent to
your request.

QUESTION

Are payments made in connection with the fact sheet you
submitted with your request for advice "expenditures" as defined
in the Act?

CONCLUSION

Since the payments for the costs for the production and
distribution of the communication are expenditures, once your
group has raised $1,000 in contributions you will qualify as a
committee.

FACTS

You have submitted a fact sheet pertaining to a local measure
which will increase taxes. You intend to distribute the fact
sheet and also plan on hiring a consultant to help coordinate your
activity and perform surveys. You are writing on behalf of a
"yet-unnamed group."

DISCUSSION

Section 82013 of the Act provides:

"Committee" means any person or combination of
persons who directly or indirectly does any of the
following:

(a) Receives contributions totaling one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a
calendar year.

(b) Makes independent expenditures
totaling one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more
in a calendar year; or

(c) Makes contributions totaling ten
thousand dollars ($10,000) or more in a
calendar year to or at the behest of
candidates or committees.

Our File No. I-92-298
Page 3

A person or combination of persons that
becomes a committee shall retain its status as a
committee until such time as that status is
terminated pursuant to Section § 84214.

The Act defines "expenditure" as:

"Expenditure" includes any monetary or non-
monetary payment made by any person...that is used
for communications which expressly advocate the
nomination, election or defeat of a clearly
identified candidate or candidates, or the
qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly
identified ballot measure.

(1) "Clearly identified" has the
following meaning:

* * *

(C) A measure that has qualified to
be placed on the ballot is clearly
identified if the communication states a
proposition number, official title or
popular name associated with the measure.
In addition, the measure is clearly
identified if the communication refers to
the subject matter of the measure and
either states that the measure is before
the people for a vote or, taken as a
whole and in context, unambiguously
refers to the measure.

(D) A measure that has not
qualified to be placed on the ballot is
clearly identified if the communication
refers to the subject matter of the
measure and to the qualification drive.

* * *

(2) A communication "expressly
advocates" the nomination, election or defeat
of a candidate or the qualification, passage
or defeat of a measure if it contains express
words of advocacy such as "vote for," "elect,"
"support," "cast your ballot," "vote against,"
"defeat," "reject," "sign petitions for" or
otherwise refers to a clearly identified
candidate or measure so that the
communication, taken as a whole, unambiguously
urges a particular result in an election.

Regulation 18225(b) (Emphasis
added).

Our File No. I-92-298
Page 4
"1


     You have submitted a fact sheet which provides information
concerning a hospital tax measure that will be on the San Luis
Obispo County ballot for the November election. The fact sheet
identifies the Hospital Tax Measure that will appear on the
November ballot. You also intend to hire a consultant to assist
your group in defeating the measure.

     As set forth above, since your fact sheet is intended to
encourage voters to vote against the measure, payments for the
fact sheet would qualify as expenditures. Consequently, once your
group has raised $1,000 for these expenditures, it will qualify as
a recipient committee under the Act and be subject to the campaign
disclosure provisions of the Act.³

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.⁴

                                   Sincerely,

                                   Scott Hallabrin
                                   Acting General Counsel
                                   [signature]
                                   By: John W. Wallace
                                       Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa




³     For guidance with respect to your specific filing requirements
you may contact our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division at
(916) 322-5662.

⁴     Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
